mechanism_narrative: A higher level of the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, reducing the present value of equities and leading to negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If rolling 2-year rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day returns is positive or insignificant (|rho|<0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a threshold of 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic slowdown, leading to negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical recession periods have often been preceded by UNRATE rising above 5.5%
failure_condition: If forward returns in triggered instances are positive in >=60% of cases, or rank IC <0.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are strong but housing starts (HOUST) are weak, it suggests consumer spending is fueled by credit rather than sustainable income growth, increasing vulnerability to a downturn and negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1% and HOUST month-over-month growth < -2%
failure_condition: If the joint signal does not produce negative average forward returns over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The predictive sign of industrial production (INDPRO) growth flips with the economic cycle: during expansions (IP above 12-month moving average), positive growth is positive for equities; during contractions (IP below 12MA), negative growth exacerbates weakness. This hypothesis focuses on the expansionary regime where direction is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical business cycles show correlation between IP growth and equity returns differs by phase
regime_hint: industrial production above 12-month moving average (expansionary phase)
failure_condition: If in IP-above-12MA regime, forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand and corporate revenue momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 1-month rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 5-day returns is negative or below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year above 3% prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and suppressing equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in triggered periods, forward returns are positive in >50% of cases, or average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth and unemployment rate (UNRATE) reveals labor market quality: if PAYEMS increases but UNRATE also rises, it suggests labor force growth outpacing job creation, a sign of labor market slack that is negative for corporate earnings and forward S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100,000 AND UNRATE month-over-month increase > 0.1%
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to produce negative average 60-day returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and broader economic health, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 12-month rolling rank correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year period.
